167504.explanation: From Vraagwijzer there will be more attention to the connection between the villages and the social domain. An additional connector is crucial here. This does not necessarily have to be a separate village coach for each core, it can also be one person.

167506.explanation: In the areas of youth, Participation Act, and Social Support Act this is, for example, necessary and legally required (in youth care). The own identity must be preserved and care/services should remain close to the resident. A small organization like Woensdrecht is vulnerable in this respect.

167509.explanation: If the nitrogen lock is lifted, extra capacity will be needed anyway. In addition, current initiators of housing construction must be helped to realize their plans within the current nitrogen rules.

167513.explanation: Not in favor of night openings due to noise nuisance and unrest. Openings are more than sufficient for the shops in our villages at this moment. Our residents also live in the villages for their peace and quiet.

167514.explanation: Supporter of strategic land purchases so that the municipality's wishes can be fulfilled and we are not dependent on third parties.

167515.explanation: We must cherish our polders and that includes agricultural land. But with good cooperation with the adjoining nature.

167516.explanation: It is the providers' responsibility to offer the best deals in the countryside.

167518.explanation: Only increase up to the maximum of the applicable inflation correction.

167520.explanation: Because of the small number of rental homes, it is already difficult to meet this target.

167521.explanation: The same rules apply to every Dutch person, regardless of religion and origin.

167522.explanation: A lot of money is already being spent on language lessons, children's education, and integration courses. Various organizations provide support for this. There are no bottlenecks.

167523.explanation: As long as there is no nuisance (as is currently the case), this should not be a problem. The necessity to keep it open after May 2027 is not yet known. We can only assess this in May 2027.

167524.explanation: Status holders are distributed among the villages. The refugees in Putte are housed in a good location and should not be dispersed. The same applies to the Ukrainian refugees in Ossendrecht. Security and supervision are easier to organize in concentrated locations.

167525.explanation: The number of labor migrants must be reduced in accordance with the revised municipal policy.

167526.explanation: Municipal decisions on this have already been made and we will not revisit them. Our municipality also has an obligation to house labor migrants. This is more manageable in a clustered facility than when labor migrants live separately in the village centers.

167527.explanation: This cannot be monitored and cannot be included in regulations.

167528.explanation: Costs too much and the investment is disproportionate to the intended goal.

167529.explanation: This aligns with our climate goals and greening of public spaces.

167530.explanation: We must encourage horeca and shop owners to make their toilets available to people in urgent need.

167531.explanation: No extra money, but we do want to support and facilitate local journalism so they can perform their work well in Woensdrecht.

167533.explanation: Swimming lessons are not part of the educational programs and parents are responsible for this themselves.

167534.explanation: Heritage clubs can also be given more responsibility. We want to preserve churches and other iconic buildings in our villages, and in the coming administrative period, we especially want to focus on documenting intangible heritage. Is more capacity needed for this?

167535.explanation: The number of BOAs (special enforcement officers) we currently employ should be sufficient. Safety, however, remains important.

167536.explanation: After 9:00 PM, the responsibility lies with the police.

167538.explanation: This is established in the Housing Deal and applicable to Woensdrecht to help more young people and other urgent housing seekers obtain a home.

167542.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for vacationers. The housing shortage must be solved in another way.

167543.explanation: If this fits within the Environmental Plan, we should not oppose it.

167544.explanation: This provision (card) is not needed because discounts can be obtained via Stichting Leergeld and ISD's webshop.

167545.explanation: For this, we have the incentive scheme that ABZ wants to maintain.

167546.explanation: All possibilities must be explored to get people out of social assistance and help them step into paid work or otherwise participate in our society.

167547.explanation: Companies are responsible for the internship compensation.

167548.explanation: All possibilities must be explored to get people out of social assistance and help them step into paid work or otherwise participate in our society.

167549.explanation: In busy shopping areas, space must be available for cyclists and pedestrians. But there must also be sufficient parking. Parking spaces can be created within the shopping area, but also just outside it: within walking distance of the shops.

167550.explanation: Agreed, with attention paid to delivery traffic and shops that depend on passers-by coming to shop.

167551.explanation: It is important to establish connections between Bergen op Zoom and Aviolanda (+ de Kooi) and between Bergen op Zoom and Zandvliet in the form of fast cycling paths that link to the Brabant network.

167552.explanation: Traffic lights do not help in these incidental situations.

167554.explanation: ABZ supports youth meeting places, but these should be realized in the villages where there is demand. Youth must embrace these facilities and ensure they remain sustainable.

167556.explanation: Every resident must be treated equally; no one should be left out, and everyone must be able to participate in our society. Therefore, we do not make exceptions for certain target groups.

167504.explanation: Within the municipality, there are already many contact points and signaling functions managing this across all villages. A separate village coach per village would cost too much and add little to the existing options.

167506.explanation: Collaboration with other municipalities offers economies of scale. This way we can share costs, pool knowledge, and maintain a strong negotiating position. The municipality remains independent but benefits from more efficient services.

167509.explanation: There must always be enough civil servants to process permit applications quickly and efficiently. When a solution for nitrogen comes for our municipality, extra capacity must certainly be allocated to handle all permit applications.

167513.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have the freedom to make their own choices. It remains advisable for entrepreneurs to make joint agreements. This promotes cooperation and contributes to an improved streetscape.

167514.explanation: The municipality can play a more active role here to retain more control within its own boundaries. Wise choices must be made and there must be no speculation with public funds.

167515.explanation: As CDA Woensdrecht, we stand behind our agricultural entrepreneurs and the agricultural entity of our municipality. Agricultural entrepreneurs are given freedom, provided we comply with legal frameworks.

167516.explanation: We leave this choice to the residents themselves. Letting the municipality bear the full costs would be too expensive. Residents in rural areas also have other options for internet and television access.

167518.explanation: As the municipality of Woensdrecht, we have been applying this policy for several years. We want to continue doing so.

167520.explanation: We already apply this in accordance with the Dispersion Act and agreements within the region.

167521.explanation: As CDA Woensdrecht, we prefer to give priority to our own residents, provided that we comply with legal frameworks.

167522.explanation: What we can do legally, we already do within the municipality. As CDA Woensdrecht, we are open to improvements to better integrate newcomers, but this does not always have to involve extra costs.

167523.explanation: We want to continue to comply with the Dispersion Act at a location that has little impact on the livability of a village. A location was found in Putte at the time. That does not mean it always has to remain in Putte.

167524.explanation: Concentrating asylum reception at one or two locations limits nuisance for villages. Guidance, supervision, and facilities can be better arranged there. Spreading across all centers increases the chance of nuisance, unrest, and pressure on small village facilities and reduces support.

167525.explanation: There are already restrictions in place based on established policy. We want to adhere to those.

167526.explanation: All political parties have already agreed to this proposal. This has been a measure to remove labor migrants from the centers, including Putte. This creates calm in the center and there is more control over labor migrants at a remote location.

167527.explanation: The CDA stimulates entrepreneurship and does not want to discourage entrepreneurs in advance with restrictive measures. We abide by legal regulations and entrepreneurs must do the same.

167528.explanation: There is already a passage under the A4. Perhaps it can be ecologically designed with some small adjustments.

167529.explanation: This keeps the number of trees in our municipality at a sustainable level.

167530.explanation: The centers are too small-scale for this. Additionally, there is a High-Need app that entrepreneurs participate in.

167531.explanation: There are already subsidy options available that can be utilized. Local media also have their own alternative local sources of income.

167533.explanation: School swimming was abolished due to high costs, limited school time, and changing responsibilities. Parents have the primary responsibility for swimming skills, while schools focus on core tasks like language and math.

167534.explanation: A full-time heritage officer is needed to protect all our material and immaterial heritage. This preserves local heritage and allows the municipality of Woensdrecht to also benefit from this treasure.

167535.explanation: As the municipality of Woensdrecht, we have already invested by expanding from two to four enforcement officers. It is prudent to first wait for the results/evaluation before assessing if further expansion is necessary.

167536.explanation: As CDA Woensdrecht, we believe the police have the primary responsibility here. They have more powers and equipment to act during the evening and night.

167537.explanation: As CDA Woensdrecht, we oppose this because it causes a lot of nuisance in the respective center or the entire municipality.

167538.explanation: We prefer more affordable owner-occupied homes over social rental homes. Where necessary, we adhere to legal frameworks.

167542.explanation: Holiday homes should be used for recreation and not for permanent residence.

167543.explanation: As CDA Woensdrecht, we support the vision of adding at least one street in each center (preferably more). Preference is within the built-up area, but if not possible, it can also be outside the built-up area.

167544.explanation: The municipality already offers many possibilities to help our residents with limited financial means, for example with paying membership fees for a club.

167545.explanation: Helping only people with fewer means with sustainability is undesirable. The energy transition requires everyone's commitment. Broad schemes result in greater impact, faster sustainability, and prevent middle incomes from dropping out or not investing.

167546.explanation: One must provide proof of this, and the municipality must assess it.

167547.explanation: Companies themselves have freedom in this. As a municipality, we can better promote our own internships.

167548.explanation: There are many people in our municipality who are both workers and caregivers. For this reason, CDA Woensdrecht believes that people with welfare benefits should also apply for jobs.

167549.explanation: For busy shopping areas, a broader view of the area's layout is needed. This includes parking, where more parking spaces should be created to increase accessibility.

167550.explanation: Raadhuisstraat needs to be redesigned with a broader vision. This option is good but requires thorough research.

167551.explanation: We encourage a fast bike path through our municipality. It gives cyclists more space and safety.

167552.explanation: An investigation is needed into how to combat rat running in our centers. Additional traffic lights may not be the right measure as they could cause a lot of inconvenience to our own residents.

167554.explanation: All youth in our centers deserve a meeting place. The Multifunctional Centers can also be an option, but a separate place can be created as well.

167556.explanation: As CDA Woensdrecht, we find norms and values very important. Everyone counts in our municipality. A separate status is not necessarily a must for us.

167504.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht considers it important that citizens can easily obtain advice. They should receive solicited and unsolicited advice when necessary to navigate (youth) care and debts.

167506.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht supports cross-border cooperation at the administrative and official level. Every cooperation must have a clear goal. Cooperation should be reconsidered if this goal becomes unclear. This should not lead to a municipal reorganization.

167509.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht does not see having more civil servants as a goal in itself. If promising construction projects need to proceed quickly, the municipality should cooperate with initiators. If more capacity is needed for this, we should scale up.

167513.explanation: Our villages each have their own character, and extended opening hours are not desirable. If there is broad demand among residents and businesses to extend hours, we should have an extensive public debate on it.

167514.explanation: All residents from all villages within the municipality deserve to grow up and age in their village. More homes must be built in the municipality of Woensdrecht. To be less dependent on market parties, the municipality will need to adopt an active strategic land policy.

167515.explanation: Farmers are an important part of businesses in Woensdrecht. They need their agricultural land for this. D66 Woensdrecht supports nature-inclusive agriculture where farmland and nature go hand in hand. We need to work together on this.

167516.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht supports good internet coverage. The vast majority of the municipality of Woensdrecht is equipped with fiber optic. Since this connection, sufficient alternatives have been developed to access good internet.

167518.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is not a cash cow. People's expenses are already high enough. Before the municipality increases charges (such as property tax), potential budget cuts should first be reviewed.

167520.explanation: Woensdrecht takes its responsibility. With asylum reception in Putte and Ukraine reception in Ossendrecht, a safe place has been created for people in need. This was done in a way that fits within Woensdrecht and is manageable regarding reception. These are exemplary locations showing how it can be done.

167521.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht supports a target group policy regarding social rental housing. We give priority to our own youth, urgency declarations for divorce situations, and status holders for social rental homes.

167522.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht finds it important that newcomers quickly feel at home and are integrated into our municipality. We want people to quickly become a full part of our villages. This strengthens community life and solidarity in our municipalities.

167523.explanation: Woensdrecht takes its responsibility regarding asylum reception, currently in Putte. If the need remains high, D66 Woensdrecht believes we must be willing to continue this. Woensdrecht shows that it can be done!

167524.explanation: The reception in Putte shows that the size used is appropriate. Due to its size, there are sufficient good facilities and the reception is not too large to be part of society. We see no reason to scatter reception over multiple centers.

167525.explanation: Woensdrecht is now a residential municipality. More labor migrants live here than work here. The limit has been reached. We believe that we should not accommodate more labor migrants than those already planned.

167526.explanation: Labor migrants who work and live in our municipality deserve good housing. To prevent pressure on residential areas in the villages, the municipality decided a few years ago to allow one large-scale reception location. This permit has already been granted.

167527.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht wants to encourage shops and companies to obtain certifications for safe, socially responsible, and sustainable business practices. However, making this mandatory goes too far.

167528.explanation: D66 wants nature reserves to be connected (ecological corridors) so that animals and plants can safely spread and reproduce. The construction of an eco-duct near Mattenburgh is seen by D66 as the right way to achieve this.

167529.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht stands for a green and attractive living environment. Trees play an important role against drought, heat stress caused by climate change, and as habitats for animals.

167530.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht believes that public toilets should be available in all population centers.

167531.explanation: Investing in local journalism is important for informing residents and also to provide a healthy counterbalance to politics. D66 supports allocating extra funding for independent journalism.

167533.explanation: Swimming promotion can be better addressed in another way. Schools are already burdened with many demands from the government. As a municipality, we must ensure that swimming certification is accessible to everyone in Woensdrecht.

167534.explanation: Woensdrecht has much heritage, both tangible and intangible. According to D66, heritage and culture should be better told and connected. The history of Woensdrecht must not be lost.

167535.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht supports further expanding the municipal enforcement officer (BOA) capacity. Safety, enforcement, and livability are priority areas for D66.

167536.explanation: D66 stands for a livable Woensdrecht. We want residents to feel safe both inside and outside the population centers. BOAs should be deployable after 9 PM to this end.

167537.explanation: The municipality must facilitate a healthy lifestyle. D66 favors an approach based on scientific insights to prevent substance use (alcohol, tobacco, and drugs) among youth and improve their well-being. A coffee shop does not fit this approach.

167538.explanation: All residents in our villages deserve to grow up and grow old in their village. Therefore, each center must have a complete housing range, including 30% social rental homes.

167542.explanation: Holiday homes were created for one purpose: recreation, not permanent residence. They often do not comply with building regulations or are located in sensitive areas, such as Natura 2000. We want to use these areas only for recreational purposes.

167543.explanation: Woensdrecht is the green municipality on the Brabantse Wal. Expansion of housing and employment must not lead to hardening of the countryside.

167544.explanation: A healthy mind in a healthy body is important. Therefore, D66 Woensdrecht believes that sports and cultural policies should focus on supporting people and associations so everyone can participate.

167545.explanation: Sustainable building is the standard within the municipality of Woensdrecht. We assist people who have financial difficulties. Additionally, D66 supports helping residents with homes having low energy labels to make their homes more sustainable.

167546.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht supports keeping people engaged in society. People should be encouraged to undertake appropriate activities according to their abilities. If this is done through volunteer work, that is sufficient.

167547.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht wants the municipality to strive to create more learning/work placements. Cooperation between education, business, and the municipality is crucial. Extra placements in sectors with shortages offer opportunities to address those shortages.

167548.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht believes people should not have to apply for jobs if they provide informal care to someone close to them (e.g., a family member). We assume that this caregiving requires so much time that working reasonably is not possible.

167549.explanation: Parking spaces and car traffic compromise the safety of cyclists and pedestrians. D66 Woensdrecht wants cyclists and pedestrians to feel comfortable and safe moving within and between villages. Therefore, in road network redesigns, we want to give cyclists more space.

167550.explanation: The Raadhuisstraat in Hoogerheide is a dangerous street with many functions. D66 wants Raadhuisstraat to become a true shopping street where cars are present but as guests.

167551.explanation: D66 wants to work with neighboring municipalities to create a 'fast cycle route': wider bike paths for longer distances. This will facilitate bicycle commuting and ensure students can cycle safely to school.

167552.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to keep seeking alternative solutions for cut-through traffic problems in Putte, Ossendrecht, Huijbergen, and Calfven. Think creatively, for example by installing traffic control measures during peak times.

167554.explanation: D66 Woensdrecht wants to invest in youth workers, youth meeting places, and sports facilities to offer young people positive activities and stimulate social contact. Also, to reduce nuisance.

167556.explanation: Attention to LGBTI emancipation is too limited. D66 Woensdrecht wants the municipality to become a rainbow municipality. Not only symbolically by flying the rainbow flag to show support for LGBTI+ persons but also incorporating this in policy and engaging in dialogue.

167504.explanation: Care must fit the person, not the other way around. Therefore, AKT wants one point of contact for care questions per center or area. Support must suit the person’s situation, with attention to the story behind the question.

167506.explanation: For AKT, cooperation must never mean that Woensdrecht loses control. The municipality remains responsible. Tasks can be shared if the municipality’s identity remains safeguarded. Otherwise, cooperation is unacceptable.

167509.explanation: AKT wants the municipality to take charge: we decide where, what, and how is built. No endless waiting, but action. The civil service capacity is not the problem.

167513.explanation: AKT considers the current permitted store opening hours from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM more than sufficient for a municipality like Woensdrecht.

167514.explanation: For AKT, own residents come first. The municipality must determine what portion of new housing is for Woensdrecht residents. The municipality must also be more active in buying and using land for housing.

167515.explanation: AKT will continue to support farmers in their business operations. More nature areas lead to permanent stagnation in housing development.

167516.explanation: In the past, the municipality made mistakes, so not everyone can access fiber-optic connections under the same conditions (no additional payment) as originally arranged. AKT supports a catch-up effort.

167518.explanation: AKT believes tax increases should only occur when well explained. Property tax (OZB) should not become a cash cow.

167520.explanation: AKT is against an asylum seekers' center (AZC) in our municipality. Those who want to return voluntarily will receive clear information and help from the municipality.

167521.explanation: For AKT, own residents come first. They have a bond with our municipality and strengthen livability.

167522.explanation: For AKT, no more spending than legally necessary. For status holders, we arrange good reception and quick guidance to housing, language, and work.

167523.explanation: AKT opposes an AZC in our municipality. With recurring extensions, this becomes a permanent location. The Distribution Act will not help us here.

167524.explanation: As long as reception is well arranged and does not cause nuisance, AKT finds a central location easiest to manage. Multiple locations cause unrest due to relocations between sites.

167525.explanation: AKT believes short-term stays should take place as much as possible in residential hotels or other lodging locations. Regular houses are for residents with a sustainable bond. The number of labor migrants is already too high in relation to the municipality's population.

167526.explanation: AKT believes short-term stays should take place as much as possible in residential hotels or other lodging locations. Regular houses are for residents with a sustainable bond. The number of labor migrants is already too high in relation to the municipality's population.

167527.explanation: Companies that are good for people and nature receive full support from AKT. We are open to attracting companies that reuse materials and operate cleanly and smartly. New polluters are not welcome. Existing polluters receive support for sustainable solutions.

167528.explanation: For AKT this is not a necessity. This is not a municipal road.

167529.explanation: AKT considers replanting desirable only where possible. A mandatory replanting obligation goes too far for AKT.

167530.explanation: For people with stomach, liver, or intestinal problems, a nearby toilet is essential. There are still too few accessible toilets. This not only limits the mobility of this group but also their participation in daily life. AKT wants sufficient accessible toilets in all communities.

167531.explanation: Strong local media connect our villages. The local broadcaster, newspapers, and online platforms show what is happening. They cover sports, culture, and associations and tell stories from all communities. AKT advocates for recognizable, accessible, and independent local reporting.

167533.explanation: Good education nearby is important for every child. AKT advocates for primary schools that are nearby and safely accessible – preferably within the community itself. We choose the human scale, with attention to physical education and swimming lessons.

167534.explanation: Heritage connects generations and shows where we come from. That is why AKT focuses on preservation, experience, and passing it on to the future by implementing Heritage Policy. For this, AKT wants to appoint at least one full-time official.

167535.explanation: AKT wants more permanent enforcement officers and at least four neighborhood police officers for the entire municipality. Safety is not only about statistics but especially about the feeling people have. That means well-maintained roads, good lighting, tackling nuisance, and short lines of communication with enforcers.

167536.explanation: AKT wants more permanent enforcement officers and at least four neighborhood police officers for the entire municipality. Expanding capacity makes it possible to achieve 24-hour coverage.

167537.explanation: We address vandalism, illegal dumping, and drug use with a zero-tolerance policy. For AKT, this also means: no coffee shops.

167538.explanation: Housing construction is too important to leave solely to the market. AKT wants to develop a realistic housing vision per community, based on local housing needs. With clear frameworks for location choice, sustainability, and affordability. This results in a percentage of housing need per community.

167542.explanation: AKT wants to legalize permanent residence in existing recreation parks with a dual designation (recreation and living) if the dwellings comply with the Building Regulations.

167543.explanation: AKT wants the municipality to take control: we determine where, what, and how things are built. No endless waiting, but action. That is why every community deserves a realistic and targeted housing vision. Plans outside the built-up area that contribute to livability and residential enjoyment should be able to proceed without delay.

167544.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. AKT advocates for preventing and combating poverty with early detection, customized solutions, and simple, workable access to help. A Woensdrecht Pass is one of the possible tools for this.

167545.explanation: AKT assists residents and associations who want to make their homes or buildings more sustainable and pays extra attention to people who struggle with their energy bills.

167546.explanation: Informal caregivers are the foundation of our healthcare system. AKT wants to continue to support them well, with respite care, practical courses (for example, dealing with dementia), and customized support when financial worries are an issue. Volunteers receive support so they can do their work well and with pleasure.

167547.explanation: AKT wants to establish an incentive fund for this. Woensdrecht companies receive compensation when they offer internships to Woensdrecht students. This contributes to young people’s connection to Woensdrecht and increases the chance they will continue to live and work here.

167548.explanation: Informal caregivers are the foundation of our healthcare system. AKT wants to continue to support them well, with respite care, practical courses, and customized support when financial worries are an issue. Exemption from job application obligations fits within this.

167549.explanation: According to AKT, the current availability of parking spaces is sufficient. A spot right at the door is not always guaranteed but is available nearby.

167550.explanation: AKT does not see this as the solution to what many view as a street with unsafe situations. AKT does believe that something must be done to improve the situation.

167551.explanation: AKT wants all bike paths checked for safety and comfort. As more people use electric bikes, bike paths must be adapted. Where necessary, they will be widened, illuminated, or refurbished. Routes to school, work, and healthcare get priority. A fast cycle path is not a must.

167552.explanation: AKT believes traffic measures should suit the road’s use. There must be more enforcement against speeding, and 30 km/h zones must be clearly and safely designed.

167554.explanation: We continue to see multifunctional centers as central meeting places. Studies are underway into youth needs for alternative uses. AKT is open to the outcomes of this research.

167556.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. For AKT, this really means everyone, and extra attention for specific groups in this form misses the point.

167504.explanation: We find this inefficient. This function should be located in the town hall where all expertise is. There should be a separate counter for it and an easy, low-threshold way to make contact via the website for specific questions.

167506.explanation: This is solely to work more effectively and efficiently, as we currently do with, for example, taxes. However, Woensdrecht must remain an independent municipality administratively at all times, as with joint arrangements and overarching agreements.

167509.explanation: Before we start this, it must first be demonstrated that there is a necessity. New national policy should be translated into local policy with less regulation and thus less workload for officials. Nitrogen regulations seem to be the biggest bottleneck.

167513.explanation: We are a liberal party and believe that entrepreneurs should decide this for themselves as long as the entrepreneur deems it necessary and it has no negative impact on the surroundings.

167514.explanation: The municipality should not become a player in this market. The municipality can exert enough influence through permits and zoning plans on this matter. When necessary, the municipality must make use of the possibilities to make purchases.

167515.explanation: We have beautiful nature, and we cherish it. On the other hand, we have our agricultural sector, which is already under pressure and important for the municipality. We support the entrepreneurs in the agricultural sector in their initiatives and innovations.

167516.explanation: This entails disproportionately high costs and is not seen as a core task for the municipality. There are other solutions to have an internet connection in those areas.

167518.explanation: We have always stood for that. This should not become a cash cow to fill gaps in the budget. We were also against the last additional increase of the property tax. No tax increases via the property tax!

167520.explanation: There are laws for that, and we want to comply. If every municipality does this, space will arise for national shelter.

167521.explanation: Everyone is equal in this. There is no reason to give priority to this group. Moreover, there is a housing shortage for everyone, not just for this target group.

167522.explanation: Newcomers are responsible for their own growth and integration. Support is needed and should lead to work and participation in society. Research is needed into the results of integration projects. Adjust where necessary.

167523.explanation: This is a well-functioning location. Additionally, as a municipality, we maintain control before this is imposed from above. Internal safety for women remains a topic we will bring to attention.

167524.explanation: We are one municipality. We look at locations rather than village cores in this. There must be a balance. For the VVD, the core is not automatically important but the right location.

167525.explanation: We consider the number disproportionately large because not all are employed in this municipality. We understand these migrant workers are necessary. Many work outside the region. This must be solved locally, in the region where these migrant workers work, not here in the municipality.

167526.explanation: This has been widely discussed in the council and already decided. People are housed there under applicable house rules and supervision. This way, we can ensure these people have a decent place to sleep and minimize nuisance as much as possible through supervision.

167527.explanation: Every entrepreneur is responsible for that themselves and must take it. Otherwise, the law, regulations, and society will hold them accountable. In principle, every entrepreneur is welcome as long as the business complies with laws and regulations.

167528.explanation: We are against this plan. The area is too small for such a large project. There is already a wildlife tunnel and a viaduct. We see this as a money-draining project whose utility has not been demonstrated. Let's use the nitrogen reserve needed for this to build houses.

167529.explanation: There is a replanting obligation within the municipality which we comply with. We are a green municipality and take our responsibility seriously.

167530.explanation: We know there are people with those problems who are therefore limited in their freedom. At the time, we advocated for locations in the municipality through the existing 'high emergency/toilet guide app'. We still support this.

167531.explanation: Local newsletters such as those in Ossendrecht and Huijbergen are a valuable supplement for information within those village centers. Occasional support in harsh times might still be possible, but no structural funding. People must stand on their own two feet and justify their right to exist.

167533.explanation: We do not see this as a task for the municipality. This belongs to the responsibility of parents in the context of upbringing. Moreover, it is a high cost item and requires a lot of organization.

167534.explanation: A full-time civil servant can be appointed if it turns out to be necessary. However, the necessity must be demonstrated. We consider local heritage important; a good implementation of heritage policy provides opportunities in schools and the tourism sector.

167535.explanation: We have recently experienced an increase in number. Community enforcement officers mainly cover the area concerning livability. In recent years, the police have become less visible. We advocate more and targeted deployment of community enforcement officers based on pre-set objectives.

167536.explanation: This is a proposal from the VVD. Public disorder does not stop at 9 pm. In fact, if people know this, they will surely adjust their behavior accordingly. Therefore, there must be more flexibility in their work schedules, in consultation with community officers, police, and the mayor, to do their job even better.

167537.explanation: The VVD is against this. Our concerns are not only about the establishment but also about the problems it brings for livability in the municipality. Especially in the border area, this has an attractive effect and lowers the threshold to obtain it. We see this as undesirable.

167538.explanation: We want more consideration of supply and demand within the margins of the law. In Woensdrecht, the demand for social housing is much lower, making this percentage inappropriate.

167542.explanation: In times of structural housing scarcity, vacancy or underutilization is politically difficult to justify. Starters, families, and singles cannot find housing. A tolerance policy for this seems sensible to the VVD, but housing costs should remain the same.

167543.explanation: The VVD does not know why this rule needs to be made. If the background of this stance is that existing agricultural areas should not be squandered, we believe it must be carefully weighed on a case-by-case basis.

167544.explanation: Support for people with a low income is one of the VVD's core values: social justice. We believe the threshold to ask for help should be lower through better information provision. We see a physical pass as undesirable.

167545.explanation: We do not see this as a task of the municipality. This should be regulated provincially or nationally. A rental home is the responsibility of the landlord.

167546.explanation: Volunteering comes from within and should not grant privileges for other matters. Everyone is equal, so how do you measure this? We cherish all volunteers; they are the motor of our community. We do not want two 'types' of volunteers, because that will happen.

167547.explanation: We do not see this as a task of the municipality. Internships are part of education, and a municipality does not have to contribute financially. However, the municipality can encourage and mediate cooperation between companies and schools.

167548.explanation: As VVD, we embrace caregivers. We are aware this can be a heavy burden. However, we do not see it as a replacement for work, and therefore we still find a job application obligation necessary.

167549.explanation: This should have a demonstrable necessity. Note that where a parking space is created, nothing else can be placed there, which might have a greater need.

167550.explanation: The car remains a user, that is evident. Studies should investigate how we can make Raadhuisstraat safer for every road user, for example by introducing a cut-off at the intersection of Arendsberg/Flemmingstraat.

167551.explanation: The distance is so short that we do not see the necessity compared to the high costs. However, when bike paths need renovation, it should be examined whether they can be widened, with the intention of improving safety for recreational cyclists.

167552.explanation: It is a problem. As VVD, we do not see traffic lights as a solution to combat rat running, and it causes a waterbed effect.

167554.explanation: Research into the need is necessary. We will warmly support initiatives, especially in the context of citizen participation.

167556.explanation: One of VVD’s core values is equality. Every person is equal regardless of religion, origin, or orientation. Emphasizing this extra encourages stigmatization and polarization.

167504.explanation: Having a village coach in the neighborhood means help is close by and easily accessible. Early support with care, help requests, or debts prevents bigger problems and enhances equal opportunities. This coach also knows the people in the neighborhood and the organizations/institutions, enabling tailored solutions.

167506.explanation: We find it important to work more and better with other municipalities to tackle major issues such as the nitrogen crisis, housing construction halt, dispersal of labor migration, poverty reduction, and enforcement.

167509.explanation: We see customized solutions as one way to revive housing construction. To achieve this, we need more civil servants focused on housing construction, permit applications, and nature conservation.

167513.explanation: Extending opening hours mainly benefits large players. For smaller companies, competing becomes more difficult. Costs for personnel, electricity, and pressure on wellbeing will also increase.

167514.explanation: By purchasing land themselves, the municipality retains control over housing construction. This way, it can counter speculation, enforce affordable housing, and build in the public interest rather than for maximum profit.

167515.explanation: Nature development is necessary to address nitrogen problems, restore biodiversity, and cope with climate change. By converting agricultural land into nature where needed, we invest in a healthy, green living environment and a future-proof municipality.

167516.explanation: Good facilities such as reliable internet are important. We will engage with telecom companies to provide fiber optic connections in all areas as quickly as possible.

167518.explanation: Financial predictability and affordability are important. By increasing property tax only in line with general price rises, purchasing power is preserved and unnecessary housing cost increases are prevented.

167520.explanation: Shelter is not a matter of minimum or maximum obligations but of humanity, capacity, and solidarity. By collaborating regionally and opting for small-scale, good shelter, Woensdrecht can take responsibility without losing sight of livability.

167521.explanation: Giving priority to groups of housing seekers pits groups against each other. We choose to increase the supply of social rental homes and invest in humane shelter and integration so everyone gains perspective without creating inequality.

167522.explanation: More investment in integration ensures newcomers can participate in society more quickly. Good guidance prevents misunderstandings and nuisance; on the other hand, it strengthens mutual understanding and contributes to a safe, livable, and solidary municipality for everyone.

167523.explanation: We consider accommodating asylum seekers very important and necessary. The location in Putte is successful and has the support of residents. This cannot be said about the shelter for labor migrants and (former) addiction care. What is successful in Putte, we must continue.

167524.explanation: Dispersing asylum accommodation prevents one village from being overburdened. Small-scale shelter across all centers creates more support, better integration, and preservation of livability.

167525.explanation: Labor migrants who contribute to the local economy are important to all of us. The problems are not necessarily in numbers but in poor housing and lack of control. By focusing on humane housing, registration, and enforcement, livability is maintained.

167526.explanation: A large-scale migrant hotel does not fit with small-scale, humane shelter. Additionally, the pressure on Putte is high, and the location does not match the community’s support.

167527.explanation: Sustainability is a core value for GL-PvdA Woensdrecht. Precisely because of this, we choose high standards, active support, and the 'polluter pays' principle. New companies established here should work sustainably, but we do not support a strict requirement.

167528.explanation: An ecoduct enhances biodiversity and restores the connection between natural areas. This aligns with efforts for nature restoration, protection of vulnerable areas, and reducing the negative effects of infrastructure on flora and fauna.

167529.explanation: The green municipality on the Brabantse Wal must remain green! One way to manage this green space responsibly is by requiring the replanting of trees.

167530.explanation: Public toilets increase accessibility and livability in all villages. They make the village pleasant to be in and usable for everyone. In addition, we want to focus on better usage of the Hogenood app by institutions and companies.

167531.explanation: Strong local journalism is important for a well-informed society. We believe it is not the municipality's role to support this with extra funding, as financial dependence can put the transparency and independence of journalism under pressure.

167533.explanation: Since school swimming stopped, swimming skills have declined. It is important that children learn to swim structurally through school, regardless of the willingness or financial situation of the parents. Therefore, we strive to introduce school swimming at least once a month starting from group 3.

167534.explanation: We value local heritage and want to make it more visible, but see no need for a separate full-time heritage officer. More collaboration and smart use of existing staff are more important than expanding personnel.

167535.explanation: We support having more municipal enforcement officers to tackle nuisance and misconduct. Additional BOAs ensure greater safety and better enforcement in neighborhoods. As far as we are concerned, the BOAs who are currently temporary should become permanently appointed.

167536.explanation: Enforcement should take place when nuisance and insecurity occur. That is not only on weekdays and during the day. Therefore, we believe BOAs should also be deployed in the evening hours so that reports can be dealt with faster and directly at the source.

167537.explanation: There is currently a lot of illegal trading via dealers in the villages of Woensdrecht. A coffeeshop provides more supervision, control, and safety. We prefer this combined with participation in the legal cannabis experiment.

167538.explanation: Woensdrecht has very few social rental homes. A good and affordable home is a right, which is why we strive for sufficient social rental homes in new construction projects so that the share of social rental housing increases and people with lower incomes can continue to live in Woensdrecht.

167542.explanation: Housing shortage is great, and for some target groups there is no suitable offer in the short term. By allowing permanent residence in holiday homes under clear conditions, we can provide temporary relief without losing sight of the need for new homes.

167543.explanation: We want to handle space and nature carefully. New construction should mainly take place within existing centers and not lead to additional damage or loss of nature.

167544.explanation: With the Woensdrecht Pass, we want to ensure that people with low incomes can participate in sports, culture, and other activities. Participation should not depend on income. The pass collects existing separate initiatives and combines them with new actions.

167545.explanation: It is important for everyone that as many people as possible can participate in sustainability. Therefore, we want to financially support residents with low incomes in making their homes more sustainable so that housing costs decrease and benefits become greater for everyone.

167546.explanation: Volunteering is valuable for society. People on social assistance who are already volunteering should not be additionally required to perform unpaid work.

167547.explanation: To keep our municipality attractive and attract enough staff, we need sufficient internship places at companies. We would like to seek cooperation with institutions and companies. However, we do not believe structural subsidies should be made available for this.

167548.explanation: Informal caregivers make an indispensable contribution and are often under pressure. People on social assistance providing informal care should therefore not be required to apply for jobs.

167549.explanation: We prioritize livability, cyclists, and pedestrians. More parking spaces in busy shopping areas do not fit with a safe and attractive public space.

167550.explanation: We want to make villages safer and more livable. In busy streets such as Raadhuisstraat, cyclists and pedestrians get priority and cars are guests.

167551.explanation: Good and safe cycling connections between centers are important. We want to invest in fast, safe, and well-lit cycling routes between all centers and between Hoogerheide and Bergen op Zoom.

167552.explanation: Additional traffic lights are not automatically the solution against rat-running. We prefer smart road design and improving livability.

167554.explanation: Meeting each other is important for welfare and prevention. Every village should have a space where young people can meet each other in a safe and accessible environment.

167556.explanation: Everyone must feel welcome and safe. By becoming a rainbow municipality, Woensdrecht shows that inclusion and equal treatment are actively supported.

statements.title: Shops should be allowed to be open from 6:00 AM to 1:00 AM.
The municipality should appoint a dedicated village coach in every centre for residents' questions about matters like (youth) care and debts.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.
A village coach is someone who supports residents when they have questions about care, welfare, housing, and participation.

titleParts.text: aanstellen voor vragen van inwoners over zaken als (jeugd)zorg en schulden.

statements.title: Raadhuisstraat in Hoogerheide should be primarily a street for cyclists and pedestrians; cars would then be guests.
Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.
More municipal staff should be hired to speed up the granting of housing permits.
The municipality should prevent agricultural land in Woensdrecht from being converted into nature areas.
There should be a Woensdrecht Pass that gives low-income people discounts on things like club memberships.
The municipality should allow permanent residence in holiday homes.
Asylum accommodation in our municipality should be distributed across all population centres.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet worden verdeeld over alle

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should support local journalism with additional funding.
The municipality should deploy BOAs after 9:00 PM as well; currently they only work during the day.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: ook na 21.00 uur inzetten. Nu werken ze alleen overdag.

statements.title: An eco-bridge should be built between the Mattemburgh estate and the forest on the east side of the A4.

titleParts.glossary: An ecoduct (or wildlife bridge) is a specially constructed overpass across a road or railway, intended to allow wild animals to cross safely and to connect fragmented natural areas.

titleParts.text: komen tussen landgoed Mattemburgh en het bos aan de oostkant van de A4.

statements.title: The municipality should abandon plans for a hotel for migrant workers near Putte.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet afzien van een

titleParts.glossary: A residential complex intended for the temporary housing of foreign workers.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Woensdrecht should be social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Woensdrecht moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: Public toilets should be installed in all population centres of the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente moeten openbare toiletten komen.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be added in busy shopping areas.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new housing development outside the built-up area.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet nieuwbouw van woningen buiten de

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Woensdrecht should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The cycle paths between Hoogerheide and Bergen op Zoom should be converted into fast-cycle routes.

titleParts.text: De fietspaden tussen Hoogerheide en Bergen op Zoom moeten

titleParts.glossary: Bicycle paths with few intersections and side paths, on which cyclists can travel long distances quickly and safely.

statements.title: Woensdrecht should become a rainbow municipality to support the LGBTQIA+ community.
People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have too little income or assets.

titleParts.text: die een familielid of bekende verzorgen als

titleParts.glossary: Someone who provides unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.

statements.title: At least one civil servant should work full time on the preservation of local heritage; currently this is done part-time.

titleParts.text: bezig zijn met het behoud van lokaal erfgoed. Nu gebeurt dat in deeltijd.

statements.title: Each population centre in Woensdrecht should have a youth meeting place.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

titleParts.text: van Woensdrecht moet een ontmoetingsplek voor jongeren komen.

statements.title: The municipality should financially assist low-income people who want to make their homes more sustainable.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet mensen met weinig geld
helpen als zij hun woning willen verduurzamen.

statements.title: The municipality of Woensdrecht should cooperate more with other municipalities.
Additional traffic lights should be installed at the edges of the towns to prevent cut-through traffic from the highway.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

titleParts.text: moeten extra verkeerslichten komen om sluipverkeer vanaf de snelweg tegen te gaan.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) may only be increased in line with the general price rise.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.
The asylum seeker reception facility in Putte should remain open after May 2027.
The municipality should ensure that fiber‑optic internet is made available in remote areas at no extra cost.
The municipality should purchase land to gain more influence over housing construction.
People on social assistance who do volunteer work should not also be required to do unpaid work mandated by the municipality.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality of Woensdrecht should not accommodate more asylum seekers than the legally required number.
All primary school students from group 3 onwards should have swimming lessons at least once a month during school hours.
The municipality should subsidize internship positions for students at local businesses.
No more migrant workers should be allowed to move into our municipality.
